message:
fix(status): prevent false idle classification from prompt-like output during active work

Agents frequently print prompt-like characters (>, ❯) as part of
active output — code examples, progress indicators, grep results,
partial lines. The previous logic checked isAtPrompt early, before
checking activity velocity, which caused actively-working agents
to be classified as idle whenever their output happened to end with
a prompt-like pattern.

unified.go:
- Move the isAtPrompt → StateIdle check to after the activity-
  velocity checks instead of before. This ensures that recent
  high-velocity output is classified as StateWorking regardless
  of prompt-like trailing characters
- Only trust isAtPrompt as an idle signal when velocity has
  genuinely settled (borderline activity threshold)
- Remove the blanket "known agent types default to idle when
  indeterminate" fallback — this caused false idle readings that
  led operators to send redundant prompts and miss that agents
  were still working. Known agents now return StateUnknown when
  state cannot be determined, matching user/shell pane behavior

unified_test.go:
- New test case: "prompt-like output with recent activity is
  working not idle" — verifies grep output containing > is
  classified as StateWorking when activity is 2s ago
- Updated test: "known agent type defaults to unknown when
  indeterminate" — assertion changed from StateIdle to
  StateUnknown to match the new conservative behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
